context.summary: Make this fun two-player game using the BBC micro:bit芒聙聶s radio feature.
how_it_works: This is a game for two people. One person hides an object and gives the seeker clues: 芒聙聵hot芒聙聶 when they are close to the object, 芒聙聵colder芒聙聶 when they are moving further away, and 芒聙聵warmer芒聙聶 when they are getting closer.

Usually, this game is played by shouting the words 芒聙聵hot芒聙聶, 芒聙聵colder芒聙聶 and 芒聙聵warmer芒聙聶 but in this version of the game the words are sent by radio from one micro:bit to another and appear on the LED display.
There are two programs, one for the hider and one for the seeker.
First, each program sets the radio group to 47. Groups are like channels, so any micro:bit using the same group will get the message. You can use any group number you like from 0-255.
The hider presses button A to send the message 芒聙聵warmer芒聙聶, presses button B to send the message 芒聙聵colder芒聙聶 and presses buttons A and B together to send the message 芒聙聵hot芒聙聶.
The micro:bit can send messages as numbers or text. We芒聙聶re using text messages in this project, so make sure you use radio blocks that refer to脗 strings脗 rather than脗 numbers脗 or脗 values.
In computing, a string is a sequence of characters that can contain letters, numbers, symbols, and spaces.
When the seeker's micro:bit receives a radio message, it shows the string received on its LED display. The strings are also displayed on the hider芒聙聶s micro:bit.
improve_it: Edit the program so that different words appear instead of 'hot', 'colder' and 'warmer'. For example, you could use the words for 'hot', 'colder' and 'warmer' in a foreign language you are learning.
Add sound effects to the program.
Use this game with a timer or step counter program to see how quickly you found the object.

code.source:
# Imports go at the top
from microbit import *
import radio
radio.config(group=47)
radio.on()

while True:
    if button_a.is_pressed() and button_b.is_pressed():
            radio.send('HOT!')
            display.scroll('HOT!')
    elif button_a.is_pressed():
        radio.send('warmer')
        display.scroll('warmer')
    elif button_b.is_pressed():
        radio.send('colder')
        display.scroll('colder')
    sleep(100)

# Imports go at the top
from microbit import *
import radio
radio.config(group=47)
radio.on()

while True:
    message = radio.receive()
    if message:
        display.scroll(message)
    sleep(100)
